_readme: Live machine-readable snapshot for the agent team. The Orchestrator is the default sole writer. Other agents submit update envelopes in .agents/updates/ and append-only artifacts in .agents/sessions/ and .agents/gates/.
_state_write_policy: Normal path: non-orchestrator agents emit update envelopes; the Orchestrator merges them into STATE.json. Direct writes are reserved for the Orchestrator or an explicit compare-and-swap emergency path.

prompt_versions._note: Short SHA-256 hash (first 12 hex chars) of the normalized prompt file contents. Run prompt-hash.py --write-state to refresh after editing any prompt file.

task_tree._note: Optional nested planning structure for multi-feature releases. Node IDs should follow _id_formats.task_node.

core_stability._note: Tracks stability of core interfaces. IN_PROGRESS means feature agents should block. STABLE means safe to build against.

features._note: Each feature entry tracks lifecycle, ownership, spec status, and any local runtime bundle claimed for parallel work.

_statuses.BUILDING: In active development. Feature flag required if merged before ship readiness.
_statuses.VERIFIED: Passed FUNCTIONAL and any required verification.
_statuses.SHIP_READY: Polished, verified, and ready for release queue.

_spec_statuses.INTERVIEW_NEEDED: Spec interview with human operator has not been run yet. Feature agent must NOT be assigned.
_spec_statuses.INTERVIEW_DONE: Interview complete, acceptance criteria drafted, awaiting human approval.
_spec_statuses.SPEC_APPROVED: Human operator has approved the spec. Feature agent may be assigned.

flags._note: All active feature flags. Remove the entry when the flag is fully removed from code.

gates._note: Append-only summary index. Full evidence lives in per-attempt artifacts under .agents/gates/.

[feature-name].verified_by: [agent or human - not the agent that built it]

blockers._note: Cross-agent dependencies. File here instead of editing another role's owned files.

BLOCKER-001.description: What needs to change and why
BLOCKER-001.impact: What is blocked until this resolves

design_requests._note: Lightweight non-blocking signals for shared design work. IDs should follow _id_formats.design_request.

do_not_break._note: Features or interfaces confirmed working. Regression against these is a hard stop.
do_not_break.[feature-or-interface]: Brief description of what must stay working

stale_flags_to_remove._note: Flags whose features have shipped but have not been cleaned up yet.
stale_flags_to_remove.FLAGS.EXAMPLE: Shipped YYYY-MM-DD. Remove the constant, env var, and conditionals.

environment_flag_matrix._note: Current flag state per environment. Infra owns this.
